scenario_description: A user places a map in the left orange duffel bag and a compass in the right one, then swaps their physical positions. The final question requires tracking the map's location through the swap.

turns.text: Here are my two bags before I pack them.
turns.image_description: Two identical bright orange canvas duffel bags sit side-by-side on a wooden floor. The bag on the left is empty with its zipper open. The bag on the right is also empty with its zipper open. A folded paper map lies on the floor near the left bag, and a brass compass lies near the right bag.

turns.text: Got it. They look ready for packing. What are you putting in which one?
Just put the map in the left one and the compass in the right one.
turns.image_description: The same two orange duffel bags. The bag on the left now contains the folded paper map visible inside the open zipper. The bag on the right contains the brass compass visible inside. The items are clearly placed in their respective bags.

turns.text: Okay, map in the left, compass in the right. Anything else changing?
I swapped their spots on the floor. Check this out.
turns.image_description: The two orange duffel bags are still on the wooden floor, but their positions have changed. The bag that was previously on the left (containing the map) is now physically located on the right side of the frame. The bag that was previously on the right (containing the compass) is now physically located on the left side of the frame. The contents remain unchanged; the map is inside the bag now on the right, and the compass is inside the bag now on the left.

turns.text: Looks like they moved around. Are we done moving things?
Where is the map now? A) In the bag on the left B) In the bag on the right C) On the floor D) In neither bag

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 establishes two identical bags: Left Bag and Right Bag. 2. Turn 3 shows the Map being placed into the Left Bag and the Compass into the Right Bag. 3. Turn 5 shows the physical positions of the bags swapped. The bag containing the Map (originally Left) is now positioned on the Right side of the image. The bag containing the Compass (originally Right) is now positioned on the Left side. 4. Therefore, the Map is currently inside the bag located on the right side of the frame.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must distinguish between the 'identity' of the bag (which held the map initially) and its current 'spatial position'. A model relying only on the final image (Turn 5) without remembering Turn 3 cannot know which bag holds the map because the bags are visually identical. A model failing to track the swap will incorrectly assume the map is still in the bag on the left (Option A).

_meta.scenario: Two identical orange duffel bags are sitting side-by-side. The user puts a map into the left bag and a compass into the right bag, then swaps the physical positions of the two bags. Finally, the user asks where the map is located.
